Next, handshakes. How you actually shake someone's hand says a lot about who you are in a business setting. The best handshake is your basic natural handshake. We call this the cooperators handshake. And what you do is very simple, shake the hand at waist level using the same pressure that the other person uses. You want to grip the hand firmly, but don't hold it too tightly. You don't want to squeeze their fist till they're like, nice to meet you. You want to lightly pump the other hand once or twice, then simply let it go. You want to avoid the nice to clear meat you handshake with the other person shaking like a ragdoll. That's too aggressive. And also, you want to avoid the passive wimpy handshake, which is like, hi, nice to meet you. And you're basically giving them two fingers like the dead fish handshake. And I will tell you, there's been studies on this. It sounds wacky, but there's been scientific studies that people that receive the wrong handshake, they are completely turned off as soon as the handshake is over. They're like, oh, that person's an idiot or I don't like that person. It says a lot about who you are. And believe it or not, the wimpy handshake, it doesn't mean that you're a wimp. It means that you don't care about the other person. That's what it tells the person, you know, oh, they think they're superior. They're not even worthy. I'm not worthy of getting a real handshake from them. So again, be very careful that when you shake someone's hands, don't go over, you don't go under. It's a neutral handshake, waist level, one, two pumps, then you let it go and you begin the conversation.
